channel.title: The Grant Desk - Visual & Media Arts
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Visual & Media Arts.

item.title: CRASSH 25th Anniversary Exhibition: Knowledge in a Fractured World
item.description:
Organisation: CRASSH, University of Cambridge

Location: Cambridge, United Kingdom (Alison Richard Building). Open worldwide.

Award: Exhibition from 5 October to 11 December 2026; no commission taken on sales. Free to enter.

Duration: Exhibition 5 October to 11 December 2026.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Centre for Research in the Arts, Social Sciences and Humanities (CRASSH) at the University of Cambridge marks its 25th anniversary with an open-call exhibition on the theme Knowledge in a Fractured World: how knowledge is produced, contested, and shared in a fragmented age. Accepts 2D, 3D, video, film, and sound work; a strong thematic fit for critical data and research-based practices. Open worldwide, free to enter, no commission on sales. Deadline 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Fonds 21: Kunst & Cultuur Project Grants (rolling, Netherlands)
item.description:
Organisation: Fonds 21

Location: Netherlands. Dutch foundations, associations and non-profit BVs/NVs; projects must reach a broad and/or new audience in the Netherlands.

Award: Minimum request EUR 10,000, no stated maximum; around EUR 11.5 million awarded per year across all pillars. No application fee.

Duration: Project funding; apply at least four months before the project starts.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Fonds 21 supports professional, high-quality art and culture projects by Dutch non-profit organizations (foundations, associations and non-profit BVs/NVs), across performing arts, visual arts, film, literature and interdisciplinary work, with additional pillars for arts education and youth & society. The minimum request is EUR 10,000 with no stated maximum, and roughly EUR 11.5 million is available annually. Applications are accepted year-round via the online portal (after a short eligibility snelcheck) and must be submitted at least four months before the project start date. Free-of-charge events are generally not supported; the fund expects most programming to be paid-access. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Het Cultuurfonds: Project Grants for Foundations and Associations (rolling, Netherlands)
item.description:
Organisation: Het Cultuurfonds (formerly Prins Bernhard Cultuurfonds)

Location: Netherlands. National office plus provincial chapters; projects in culture, nature and science.

Award: Typically EUR 15,000 to EUR 50,000 for national project applications by foundations and associations; smaller amounts via provincial chapters and themed funds (EUR 1,500 to EUR 25,000). Individual makers and collectives can request up to EUR 5,000. No application fee.

Duration: Project funding; decision within roughly 2 weeks to 3 months depending on the route.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Het Cultuurfonds (formerly Prins Bernhard Cultuurfonds) invests over EUR 40 million per year in culture and nature in the Netherlands. Foundations and associations can apply year-round for project grants (typically EUR 15,000 to EUR 50,000 at national level), and since 2025 individual makers and collectives can apply for up to EUR 5,000. Provincial chapters run their own quarterly rounds for regional projects, and numerous themed funds (EUR 5,000 to EUR 25,000) cover specific disciplines. Rolling intake via the online application portal. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: A.R.M. Holding / Art Dubai Projects: H Residence Sculpture Park Commission (Dubai)
item.description:
Organisation: A.R.M. Holding / HUNA / Art Dubai Projects

Location: H Residence Sculpture Park, Dubai, UAE. Open to UAE-based, regional and international artists.

Award: AED 1,000,000 commission envelope, inclusive of artist fee, design development, fabrication, materials, production, transport, installation, lighting and finishing. Proposal fees are available for shortlisted artists. No submission fee.

Duration: Proposal development September 2026; fabrication autumn 2026; unveiling 2 December 2026

Deadline: 25 Jul 2026

A.R.M. Holding and HUNA, with the commission supported and managed by Art Dubai Projects, invite artists to propose a permanent public artwork for the H Residence Sculpture Park in Dubai, the anchor piece of the park's programme. Artists respond to the theme 'We Emerge Stronger than Before' through a permanent, site-specific outdoor sculpture; the strongest proposals will find what the phrase opens up (continuity, endurance, renewal, collective presence, the long view) through material, form, scale and the conditions of the site. Where language or text is incorporated, Arabic should lead. TECHNICAL: footprint minimum around 15 sqm, up to 3 m high; weight restriction 5.5 kN/m2; must be suitable for long-term outdoor display in Dubai (summer over 40 C, humidity to 90 percent, about six hours direct sun daily); presence by day and night. ELIGIBILITY: open to artists with or without gallery representation; UAE-based, regional and international artists welcome; prior experience with permanent or large-scale outdoor/public installation required. BUDGET: AED 1,000,000 all-inclusive; shortlisted artists receive full budget parameters and proposal fees at the proposal development stage. SUBMISSION: CV and portfolio (attention to outdoor, large-scale or permanent commissions), concept statement (max 300 words), a render or visualisation in context plus at least one sketch, technical specifications, and a site response. TIMELINE: submissions close 25 July 2026; shortlist notified from 10 August; commission awarded after September proposal development; work unveiled at H Residence on 2 December 2026. Local fabrication strongly preferred. Questions: Shahad@artdubai.ae.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Fundacion Camila Caneque: International Residency Programme 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Fundacion Camila Caneque with BAR project

Location: Emporda, Catalonia, Spain. Open internationally; individuals only.

Award: EUR 2,000 gross stipend, plus accommodation, meals, utilities, round-trip travel, insurance, and curatorial support. 6 weeks. No application fee.

Duration: Residency 8 October to 20 November 2026.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Fundacion Camila Caneque, with BAR project, hosts a six-week international residency (8 October to 20 November 2026) titled In the meantime nothing happens, in the Emporda region of Catalonia. Open to artists, curators, researchers, and interdisciplinary practitioners; the residency covers a EUR 2,000 gross stipend, accommodation, meals, utilities, round-trip travel, insurance, and curatorial support. Individuals only. Deadline 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Modern Art Oxford: Collaborative Micro-commissions Open Call 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Modern Art Oxford

Location: Oxford, UK. Open to collectives, duos, partnerships and collaborative groups based in the Arts Council England South East area (Oxfordshire, Berkshire, Buckinghamshire, Hampshire and Isle of Wight, Surrey, Kent, East Sussex, West Sussex).

Award: GBP 1,000 artist fee per selected collective, plus up to GBP 500 production budget, curatorial mentorship from the Modern Art Oxford team, four months of public exhibition display, professional installation photography, promotion, technical and production support, artwork transport support within the South East, peer networking, and inclusion in a public preview event. No application fee.

Duration: Production August to November 2026; install 23 November to 4 December 2026; exhibition 5 December 2026 to April 2027

Deadline: 26 Jul 2026

Modern Art Oxford marks its 60th anniversary with an open call for three collaborative micro-commissions responding to the question: how can collective practice/s help us to embrace uncertainty and complexity? Collectives, duos, partnerships and collaborative groups based across the South East of England are invited to propose new small-scale experimental works for a group exhibition in the Ground Floor Gallery, running alongside a major solo exhibition by Mike Nelson, who is also on the selection panel. Proposals may take the form of multidisciplinary research, site-responsive work, grassroots organising, performance, visual art, sound, poetry or other creative practices; ideas should respond to the theme of collective practice and uncertainty, explore collaborative approaches, experiment across disciplines, be achievable within the budget and timeline, and be original to this open call (building on existing research is fine). WHO: groups of two or more people, including established artist collectives, newly formed collaborations, interdisciplinary partnerships, informal or community-led groups, and family collaborations, based in the Arts Council England South East area (Oxfordshire, Berkshire, Buckinghamshire, Hampshire and Isle of Wight, Surrey, Kent, East Sussex, West Sussex). SUPPORT: each of the three selected collectives receives a GBP 1,000 artist fee, up to GBP 500 production budget, curatorial mentorship, four months of exhibition display, installation photography, promotion, technical and production support, and transport support within the South East. TIMELINE: online briefing and Q&A 15 June 2026 (email creativelearning@modernartoxford.org.uk for the link); selection confirmed 14 August 2026; production August to November 2026; preview party 4 December 2026; exhibition 5 December 2026 to April 2027. Apply via Google Forms; an information pack PDF and FAQ are on the call page. DEADLINE: Sunday 26 July 2026, 23:59.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AVISTAVULCÃO: Azores Artist Residency 2027 (Faial, Portugal)
item.description:
Organisation: AVISTAVULCÃO / Barca 13

Location: Faial Island, Azores, Portugal (near the Capelinhos Volcano). Open to artists from countries eligible for the Creative Europe programme.

Award: EUR 1,350 allowance per resident for the month, plus travel allowance (EUR 400 to 800), green mobility top-up (EUR 400), visa top-up (EUR 120/resident), outermost regions and overseas territories top-up (EUR 175/resident), family top-up (EUR 200 per child/resident), and accessibility support. No application fee.

Duration: One month, March 2027

Deadline: 01 Aug 2026

AVISTAVULCÃO opens a call for a one-month artist-in-residence programme focused on research and creation, taking place in March 2027 and intended for an artist or artistic duo. Located on the island of Faial near the Capelinhos Volcano (Azores, Portugal), the residency offers immersion in a territory shaped by geology, insularity, and the interplay between nature and human presence, with the Azores treated as living material for creation. Artists are invited to develop projects engaging with landscape, memory, local history, and the contemporary challenges facing peripheral regions; suggested starting points include geological time, transformation, resilience, isolation, mobility, and the relationships between communities and the environment. Context-sensitive proposals that prioritise observation, listening and a relationship with the place are valued. Residents meet local stakeholders and share moments with the community, culminating in a public presentation of the process or results. SUPPORT (in-person mobility): EUR 1,350 allowance per resident, travel allowance of EUR 400 to 800, green mobility top-up of EUR 400, visa top-up of EUR 120 per resident, outermost regions top-up of EUR 175 per resident, family top-up of EUR 200 per child, and accessibility support. ELIGIBILITY: individual artists and duos (cross-disciplinary welcome) from countries eligible for the Creative Europe programme. DEADLINE: 1 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: JCAL: ARTWorks Fellowship 2026-2027
item.description:
Organisation: Jamaica Center for Arts and Learning (JCAL)

Location: Jamaica, Queens, New York City, USA. Open to visual artists based in New York City (preferably Queens) or Long Island; priority to Queens-based BIIPOC artists.

Award: $5,000 per Fellow, paid out over the course of the program, plus free shared studio space at JCAL (about 550 sq ft, as available), a 7 x 4 ft storage closet, one free JCAL class (space-available), an Artist Mentor and seminar series, and a concluding two-month group exhibition with catalogue in JCAL's Miller and Community galleries. No application fee.

Duration: Six-month fellowship: 7 October 2026 to 18 June 2027; exhibition opens 9 April 2027

Deadline: Closed 13 Jul 2026

Jamaica Center for Arts and Learning (JCAL) invites New York City-based visual artists to apply for ARTWorks, its Artist Fellowship Training Seminar Series: a six-month fellowship focused on career sustainability that amplifies the work of emerging, underrepresented visual artists. Six Fellows treat their practice as a small business, set entrepreneurial and professional goals, and build a long-term plan, supported by a Program Manager, an established Artist Mentor, cohort meetings, and at least six seminar events (past topics: copyright and contracts, working with galleries and collectors, grant writing, taxes and accounting, marketing, art handling). SUPPORT: $5,000 per Fellow paid over the program, free shared studio space (about 550 sq ft, as available) with a storage closet, one free JCAL class, and a concluding two-month group exhibition with catalogue (opens 9 April 2027). ELIGIBILITY: reside in New York City (preferably Queens) or Long Island; identify as BIIPOC (Black, Immigrant, Indigenous, People of Color); demonstrate clarity of concept and strong craft with a deep commitment to social practice; must NOT have received major exposure via exhibitions, grants or residencies; must not be enrolled in any college degree or certificate program; past Fellows ineligible. Priority to Queens-based artists of underrepresented communities; multidisciplinary practices welcome, but the program focuses on visual artists working in traditional forms. APPLY via Submittable with six images OR two 3-5 minute videos, a one-page work-sample description, artist statement (max 300 words), one professional reference (max 300 words), CV, and a paragraph on your expectations of ARTWorks. Jury reviews submissions without obvious identifiers. TIMELINE: first-round notifications 3 August 2026, interviews 11-14 August, Fellows notified 21 August, program runs 7 October 2026 to 18 June 2027. DEADLINE: midnight Monday 13 July 2026 (portal closes 14 July 2026, 6:00 AM ET).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Art Moves Festival: Billboard Art Competition 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Art Moves Festival

Location: Torun, Poland (works shown on city billboards). Open internationally; digital submission.

Award: Main award PLN 6,500 (approx. EUR 1,530); 8 selected works exhibited on billboards across Torun in September 2026. Free entry.

Duration: Exhibition on Torun billboards, September 2026.

Deadline: Closed 15 Jul 2026

The Art Moves Festival in Torun, Poland, runs an annual billboard art competition; the 2026 theme is Fear or courage?. Artists submit digitally, eight selected works are shown on city billboards in September, and the main award is PLN 6,500 (approx. EUR 1,530). Free to enter, open internationally. Deadline 15 July 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Mondriaan Fund: Residency
item.description:
Organisation: Mondriaan Fund (Mondriaan Fonds)

Location: Netherlands. Applicants must be based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom; residencies may take place in the Netherlands or abroad.

Award: Grant amount varies depending on the location and duration of the residency. Applicants must contribute at least 10% themselves (personal investment, third-party contributions, an interest-free loan, or crowdfunding).

Duration: Varies by residency.

Deadline: 27 Aug 2026

The Mondriaan Fund's Residency grant supports professional visual artists, curators, and art observers who want to deepen their practice through a residency in the Netherlands or abroad. It contributes to the costs of a self-arranged residency that offers meaningful development for the applicant's work. WHO CAN APPLY: visual artists, curators, and art observers who have been professionally active for at least one year and are based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom. AMOUNT: the grant amount varies depending on the residency's location and duration; applicants must contribute at least 10% of the costs themselves, through personal investment, third-party contributions, an interest-free loan, or crowdfunding. APPLYING: submitted through the Mondriaan Fund's online application portal, which requires a pre-created account (allow several working days). DEADLINE: 27 August 2026, 16:00 Dutch time (11:00 Caribbean time).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Black Arts Centre: Call for Exhibition and Programming Proposals
item.description:
Organisation: Black Arts Centre

Location: Surrey, British Columbia, Canada. Priority to Black artists, curators, and art organizers, and to projects aligned with the gallery's mandate (BIPOC, Disabled, Queer, Immigrant/Refugee/Newcomer perspectives).

Award: Successful proposals receive financial, budgeting, and curatorial support from the Black Arts Centre, including project execution, production, logistics, and marketing. No submission fee.

Duration: Exhibition proposals considered for Spring 2027 and beyond; programming proposals for projects taking place from September 2026 onward. Submission portal open 1 June to 8 August 2026; applicants begin to hear back in September.

Deadline: 08 Aug 2026

The Black Arts Centre is calling for proposals from artists for exhibitions and public programming. Exhibition proposals are considered for Spring 2027 and beyond, while programming proposals are considered for projects taking place from September 2026 onward. The submission portal is open from 1 June to 8 August 2026. Proposals are reviewed by the Black Arts Centre team and a revolving committee of external community members, evaluated using an internal rubric; applicants begin to hear back in September. SUPPORT: successful proposals receive financial, budgeting, and curatorial support from the Black Arts Centre, including project execution, production, logistics, and marketing. ELIGIBILITY AND PRIORITY: applicants do not need to identify as Black to submit, but priority is given to Black artists, curators, and art organizers, and to artists and projects that align with the gallery's mandate (BIPOC, Disabled, Queer, Immigrant/Refugee/Newcomer perspectives). Submission does not guarantee approval for this cycle, but all proposals are kept on file for future consideration, and updated or alternate proposals are accepted. Questions and PDF proposals/portfolios: info@theblackartscentre.ca. Deadline 8 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Railyard Art Project: Call for Temporary Public Art (Santa Fe)
item.description:
Organisation: Railyard Art Project (Railyard Park + Plaza)

Location: Santa Fe, New Mexico, United States (Railyard Park + Plaza, City-owned property under a Conservation Easement).

Award: USD 3,500 honorarium per project (two projects selected), plus marketing support and liability insurance. Funded by Santa Fe's Arts and Culture Commission. No application fee.

Duration: Two projects hosted between October 2026 and June 2027.

Deadline: 05 Aug 2026

The Railyard Art Project (RAP) facilitates temporary public art installations by established and emerging artists and arts collectives in Santa Fe's Railyard Park + Plaza. RAP is accepting proposals for temporary public art installations and creative projects; thanks to funding from Santa Fe's Arts and Culture Commission, two projects will be hosted between October 2026 and June 2027, each receiving a USD 3,500 honorarium plus marketing support and liability insurance. THEMES: the RAP Committee encourages collaboration across disciplines (art, science, history) and welcomes thoughtful, challenging, playful, and edifying art in the context of social and cultural expression and environmental sustainability. Proposed projects must physically withstand outdoor public display and engage the community by responding to the natural and social landscape of the park. SELECTION CRITERIA: overall artistic merit; context and familiarity with the proposed site; practical viability; and potential for community engagement and cultural/educational enrichment. Artists should select an eligible area of the park's open space and describe the project in detail; photos or renderings may be uploaded with the application (a map of Railyard Art Locations is linked from the program page). The RAP Committee, made up of community members and Park + Plaza stakeholders, selects and schedules the exhibitions; a formal in-person presentation may be requested depending on complexity. CONDITIONS: the park operates under a Conservation Easement, so installations cannot be located on planted areas, no staking of any kind is permitted (including during install/de-install), and nothing may be hung from trees. Artists lend artwork at their own risk (no protection against vandalism or weather damage, and artists are responsible for repairs). Installs and de-installs must be coordinated in advance with the RAP Committee or Railyard Park Conservancy staff, with a Committee or staff member on site throughout. Application deadline 5 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Target Margin Theater: TMT Institute Fellowship 2026-27
item.description:
Organisation: Target Margin Theater

Location: Brooklyn, New York, United States (Sunset Park). Fellows must be available in person for intensives, monthly breakfasts, and excursions.

Award: USD 2,000 stipend, plus space, material resources, and collaborators to support each Fellow's experiments. No application fee.

Duration: 2026-27 season: three intensive sessions in Brooklyn (5-8 November 2026, 8-10 January 2027, 19-21 March 2027), monthly breakfasts October 2026 through May 2027, and an Open Studio weekend in June 2027.

Deadline: 22 Jul 2026

Target Margin Theater invites applications for its next cohort of TMT Institute Fellows for the 2026/2027 season. The Institute is a cohort of artists who seek to radically disrupt and re-examine their existing practice without any goal or expected result: pure experimentation with no product-oriented end goal. Fellows pursue no project, no developmental stage, no deliverable; misfires, dead ends, and bad ideas are considered a fruitful part of the work. SUPPORT: TMT provides space, material resources, collaborators, and a USD 2,000 stipend. STRUCTURE: three short intensive sessions at Target Margin Theater in Brooklyn (5-8 November 2026, 8-10 January 2027, and 19-21 March 2027, all day with some evening hours), monthly breakfasts at Junior's in Brooklyn (20 October, 30 November, 4 January, 1 February, 8 March, 5 April, 3 May), and occasional excursions. After the second intensive, each Fellow commits to an exploration pursued outside group gatherings, supported by TMT resources. The season ends with an Open Studio weekend in June 2027 to share explorations with the community; this is not a performance and should not be an audience-attended, time-bounded show. WHO CAN APPLY: artists from any discipline within or outside theater, as well as production, technical, and administrative workers in the arts. Applicants must have an established, existing body of work and practice they are committed to changing; do not apply with a project. Recent graduates of training programs are unlikely to be appropriate candidates; no formal education level or degree is required. SELECTION: TMT staff and artistic community review applications and invite a handful of candidates to interview; all applicants are notified by October. Deadline: Wednesday 22 July 2026, 11:59 pm EST.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Arts Council of Ireland: Visual Arts Project Award 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council of Ireland / An Chomhairle Ealaion

Location: Republic of Ireland. Applicants based outside Ireland may apply if the application clearly shows how outcomes benefit the arts in the Republic of Ireland.

Award: Three strands: up to EUR 12,000 (planning and pre-production), up to EUR 60,000 (production and engagement), up to EUR 150,000 (partnership production). No application fee.

Duration: Projects delivered in 2027.

Deadline: 23 Jul 2026

The Arts Council of Ireland's Visual Arts Project Award funds professional visual artists, curators, and producers to develop and deliver visual arts projects, in three strands: planning and pre-production (up to EUR 12,000), production and engagement (up to EUR 60,000), and partnership production (up to EUR 150,000). Primarily aimed at applicants based in the Republic of Ireland, but the Arts Council's standard eligibility clause allows applicants based elsewhere where the application explicitly demonstrates how the outcomes will benefit the arts in Ireland. The wider Project Award 2027 covers nine disciplines including film and music on a similar timeline. Deadline 23 July 2026, 17:30 Irish time.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mondriaan Fund: Publication
item.description:
Organisation: Mondriaan Fund (Mondriaan Fonds)

Location: Netherlands. Featured artist(s) must be professional visual artists based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom; publishers may co-apply.

Award: Contribution of up to 35% of eligible expenses. Total budget for this round is EUR 205,000.

Duration: Project-based (production of a publication).

Deadline: 30 Jul 2026

The Mondriaan Fund's Publication grant supports the production of special publications about living visual artists from the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom. It funds books and other publications that give substantial, high-quality attention to an artist's work and practice. WHO CAN APPLY: visual artists and publishers, individually or jointly; the featured artist(s) must be professional and have been active for at least one year, based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom. AMOUNT: the Mondriaan Fund contributes a maximum of 35% of the eligible expenses; the available budget for this round is EUR 205,000. APPLYING: submitted through the Mondriaan Fund's online application portal, which requires a pre-created account (allow several working days to set up). DEADLINE: 30 July 2026, 16:00 Dutch time (10:00 Caribbean time).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Liu Shiming Art Foundation: Artist Grants 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Liu Shiming Art Foundation

Location: Open to artists of any country; application must be in English.

Award: Up to five grants of USD 5,000 each. Disbursed in two payments (USD 3,000 by 15 January 2027 and USD 2,000 on project completion). No application fee.

Duration: Project grant for new work

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

The Liu Shiming Art Foundation's annual Artist Grants (fourth edition) award up to five grants of USD 5,000 each to early-career visual artists to create new work exploring connections between traditional, cultural, or historical influences and contemporary artistic practice. ELIGIBILITY: visual artists in the first decade of their career (two to ten years practicing professionally); applicants must have received a studio-arts degree between 2016 and 2024, or had their debut gallery or juried-exhibition show between 2016 and 2026. Open to artists of any country; the application and all supporting materials must be in English. TIMELINE: applications open 1 June 2026; close 31 August 2026; recipients notified by 2 December 2026. Apply via the Foundation's grants page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Ucross Fellowship for Native American Visual Artists: Spring 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Ucross Foundation

Location: Sheridan, Wyoming, USA

Award: Fully funded fellowship residency; living accommodations, meals, and work space provided. No application fee.

Duration: Spring session (February through early June 2027); residencies range from two to six weeks

Deadline: Closed 15 Jul 2026

The Ucross Fellowship for Native American Visual Artists is a dedicated fellowship within Ucross Foundation's residency program, offering fully funded time and space to make work on a working cattle ranch in the foothills of Wyoming's Bighorn Mountains. The fellowship provides living accommodations, meals, and work space at no charge, with no application fee. One Fellowship winner is selected per session; all applicants also have the option of being considered for a general Ucross residency. ELIGIBILITY: open to Native American visual artists who are practicing contemporary artists currently producing work in any visual medium, including but not limited to painting, drawing, mixed media, sculpture, printmaking, photography, video, animation, performance art, installation, and ceramics; and who are enrolled members of a state-recognized or federally-recognized Tribe, Pueblo, Nation, Native Community, Political Entity, or Alaskan Native Village. The work sample and project description are the most significant features of the application. Unless the work is interdisciplinary, applicants are encouraged to apply in a single primary discipline and submit a work sample and project description emphasizing that discipline. Work-sample requirements: VISUAL ARTWORK, submit 10 images (JPEG format, no PDFs) with file names including title and size, plus a data sheet listing title, medium, size, and year of completion for each image; FILM/VIDEO/ANIMATION/PERFORMANCE, submit up to 3 video files (each under 8 minutes and smaller than 200 MB) or up to 3 Vimeo or YouTube links. The open call for the Spring 2027 session closes July 15, 2026 (apply by 11:59 p.m. MT). A separate Ucross Fellowship for Native American Writers is also offered.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Akademie Schloss Solitude: Web Residencies No. 22 - Ignore All Previous Instructions
item.description:
Organisation: Akademie Schloss Solitude

Location: Online (fully remote). Awarded by Akademie Schloss Solitude, Stuttgart, Germany.

Award: EUR 1,200 grant for a four-week online residency, plus a microsite to develop and publish the project, and support from a web/UX designer and a programmer in a supportive capacity.

Duration: Four-week online residency.

Deadline: 07 Aug 2026

Web Residencies No. 22, 'Ignore All Previous Instructions,' is curated by !Mediengruppe Bitnik as part of Akademie Schloss Solitude's Web Residencies program, which since 2016 has supported young talents from the international digital scene as well as artists of all disciplines engaging with web-based practices. For the tenth anniversary of the program, this edition invites artists to create works that challenge extractive digital platforms and AI systems, exploring how to interrupt dominant technological logics, from ad-tech surveillance to prompt injection vulnerabilities, and imagine alternative structures and communication methods. The open call seeks proposals that hijack, glitch, and disrupt rather than comply with existing systems, drawing on strategies like Situationist detournement to map routes through and around the grids we are trapped within. Four to six proposals will be selected. BENEFITS: a four-week online residency; a grant of EUR 1,200; a web space (microsite) on which the project can be developed and published online; support from a web/UX designer and a programmer (in a supportive capacity only, not to build an entire website); and participation in individual and group online events with the curators and Solitude fellows. Offline or hybrid formats (a reading session, panel, exhibition, or curated program) may follow. ELIGIBILITY: artists of all disciplines may apply, with no age limit; submission is free of charge. The residency takes place exclusively online, and selected fellows agree to be available online during the four weeks. TIMELINE: the open call runs from July 7 to August 7, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Alabama Historical Commission: Clotilda Memorial Public Art Commission
item.description:
Organisation: Alabama Historical Commission (with Forecast Public Art)

Location: Africatown, Mobile, Alabama, USA; open to artists internationally.

Award: USD 2,000,000 total project budget for a paid public art commission. No application fee.

Duration: Permanent public art commission

Deadline: 09 Aug 2026

The Alabama Historical Commission, working with public-art consultancy Forecast Public Art, seeks proposals for a major memorial artwork honoring the survivors of the Clotilda, the last known ship to illegally traffic enslaved Africans to the United States (1860). The memorial will convey the survivors' lives and legacies and be developed through collaborative community engagement with historic Africatown, the community founded by Clotilda survivors, and the broader diaspora. ELIGIBILITY: open to artists of all disciplines, including time-based and media artists; individuals and teams; open internationally. BUDGET: USD 2,000,000 total project budget. DEADLINE: 9 August 2026, 11:59pm CST. Applications are handled through the CODAworx RFQ platform; find the Clotilda Memorial listing in the CODAworx opportunities index. Verify the listing details on CODAworx before applying.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Prospect Art: NEW WORK Commission 2027 - "We Come in Pieces"
item.description:
Organisation: Prospect Art

Location: Remote / online. Open to visual artists worldwide; programs (ONE WORK, BROADCAST) run online.

Award: NEW WORK: USD 1,500 commission for one artist, plus a featured website section and social promotion. BROADCAST: USD 125 screening or speaking fee if selected. ONE WORK: a published article (exposure). NOTE: a USD 10 administrative fee applies to apply, with a no-questions-asked waiver available on request before the deadline. PayPal account required to receive funds.

Duration: The commissioned NEW WORK project should be completed within twelve months of selection.

Deadline: 16 Aug 2026

Prospect Art invites visual artists worldwide to apply for the 2027 NEW WORK commission, a USD 1,500 project grant supporting the creation of a new, conceptually driven work responding to the curatorial theme "We Come in Pieces," developed by 2026-2027 Curatorial Fellow Alma Sammel. Through a single application, artists are also considered for ONE WORK (an online series featuring an in-depth conversation centered on a single completed artwork) and BROADCAST (a curated online program of screenings, artist talks, and discussions). THEME: "We Come in Pieces" proposes the Alien as a method for shifting perspective, approaching alienness as a practice of estrangement to encounter the world beyond familiar human-centered narratives. It explores relationships between human and more-than-human worlds, speculative cosmologies, ecological systems, technologies, landscapes, histories, infrastructures, and nonhuman forms of intelligence (satellites and orbital debris, fungal networks, oceans, atmospheric systems, algorithmic intelligences), inviting works that displace the point of listening, seeing, sensing, or knowing. Research-based and conceptually driven practices are especially encouraged. WHAT'S OFFERED: NEW WORK, USD 1,500 to one artist plus a featured website section and social promotion (project completed within 12 months); ONE WORK, a published article and social promotion; BROADCAST, a USD 125 screening or speaking fee and inclusion in a themed program. ELIGIBILITY: professional visual artists working in any medium at any career stage, except those currently enrolled in undergraduate or graduate studio art programs; collectives may apply. Media welcomed include moving image, installation, sculpture, photography, performance, sound, digital media, social practice, and interdisciplinary forms. APPLICATION: submit via the official Google Form with contact information, a project description (250 words), a sketch/visualization (1-3 images), up to 3 links to completed work samples, a statement relating the project to the theme (200 words), a short bio and exhibition history (500 words), and a budget breakdown of the USD 1,500. A USD 10 administrative fee is paid via PayPal (include PayPal name and transaction ID); a fee waiver is available with no explanation by emailing submissions@prospectart.org before the deadline. Writers may email gioj@prospectart.org with samples. DEADLINE: 16 August 2026; results for the NEW WORK grant notified by end of September. INFO SESSION: 5 August 2026 at noon PST.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: New Emergence Art Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: New Emergence Art

Location: International; open worldwide. Shortlist shown in an online exhibition.

Award: Prize fund worth over GBP 5,000 across nine awards, including a GBP 1,000 Grand Prize cash award, a two-week NG Art Creative Residency in Provence, France (worth EUR 2,000), a GBP 300 Founder's Prize cash award, and materials or subscription awards from Staedtler UK, Rosemary & Co, GreatArt, Michael Harding, and The Art Ladder. NOTE: there is a paid entry fee.

Duration: One-off prize; shortlisted artists shown in an online exhibition 11 August to 7 September 2026

Deadline: 03 Aug 2026

The New Emergence Art Prize 2026 is an international open call for emerging artists, presented by New Emergence Art. There is no set theme and work may be submitted in any medium. AWARDS: a prize fund worth over GBP 5,000 across nine awards supporting artists through cash prizes, artist materials, mentoring, and a residency, including a GBP 1,000 Grand Prize, a two-week NG Art Creative Residency in Provence, France (worth EUR 2,000), a GBP 300 Founder's Prize cash award, and materials or subscription awards from Staedtler UK, Rosemary & Co, GreatArt, Michael Harding, and The Art Ladder. ELIGIBILITY: open internationally to emerging artists; entrants may submit up to three original artworks that represent the quality, originality and direction of their practice. Final selections are made by an independent judging panel considering concept, execution, originality and impact. Shortlisted artists are featured in an online exhibition running 11 August to 7 September 2026. NOTE: there is a paid entry fee (amount set at the entry step). DEADLINE: 3 August 2026, 23:59 BST. Apply via https://www.newemergenceart.com/apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Pollock-Krasner Foundation Grant for Artists
item.description:
Organisation: Pollock-Krasner Foundation

Location: New York, USA (grants awarded internationally)

Award: Up to USD 50,000 (unrestricted)

Duration: One-year grant period

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The Pollock-Krasner Foundation awards unrestricted grants to professional visual artists who are painters, sculptors, and artists working on paper, including printmakers. Grants range up to USD 50,000 over a one-year period and can be used for both professional and personal expenses such as art supplies, studio rent, and living costs. Applicants must demonstrate recognizable artistic merit and financial need, and must be actively exhibiting their current work in professional venues such as galleries and museums. The Foundation does not fund students, commercial or graphic art, video, performance, film, or generative and AI-generated work. Applications are accepted on a rolling, year-round basis with no deadline, and the review process typically takes nine months to a year. There is no application fee, and all applications must be submitted online.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Cloud House Residency
item.description:
Organisation: Dreamsong

Location: Minneapolis, Minnesota, USA

Award: USD 300 per week stipend (prorated), plus free accommodation in a 700 sq ft one-bedroom cottage; possible studio space and travel/materials budget on request USD 30 application fee (plus USD 500 refundable deposit upon acceptance)

Duration: 4 to 6 weeks (one-week stays available periodically)

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The Cloud House Residency, run by Dreamsong in the Northeast Arts District of Minneapolis, provides accommodation and support for individuals working in contemporary art who intend to develop, exhibit, or curate new work in Minnesota. Residents stay in a 700 square foot one-bedroom cottage on Dreamsong's compound and receive a prorated stipend of USD 300 per week, with possible studio space and travel or materials budgets considered on request. The program is open to emerging and mid-career visual artists, curators, and writers with a focus on contemporary art, and it hosts one individual or a two-person collaborative team at a time. The organizers arrange introductions to local artists, curators, writers, institutions, and galleries across the Twin Cities. Standard stays are 4 to 6 weeks, with occasional one-week options. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and the program is currently booking residents for October through December 2026 and throughout 2027.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Factory on Willow Funded Artist in Residence
item.description:
Organisation: The Factory on Willow

Location: Manchester, New Hampshire, USA

Award: Furnished studio apartment with heat, hot water, electricity and internet, plus a USD 350 travel stipend and a USD 1600 stipend toward food and art supplies

Duration: 12 weeks (about 3 months)

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The Factory on Willow is a non-profit funded artist residency in Manchester, New Hampshire, housed in a 90,000 square foot mill building from 1904. The 12 week program supports amateur and emerging artists of any medium to develop their current projects and creative practice. Selected artists receive a furnished studio apartment with utilities and internet, a USD 350 travel stipend, and a USD 1600 stipend covering food and art supplies. The residency runs in three annual sessions (March to May, June to August, and September to November), with two artists selected per session. Each residency concludes with a public capstone showcase, and participants donate one work to the Factory's permanent collection. There is no application fee and applications are accepted on a rolling basis.

Open call details

item.title: Adolph and Esther Gottlieb Foundation Emergency Grant
item.description:
Organisation: Adolph and Esther Gottlieb Foundation

Location: New York, USA (applicants worldwide)

Award: Up to USD 15,000 (USD 5,000 typical), one-time grant

Duration: One-time emergency grant

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The Adolph and Esther Gottlieb Emergency Grant provides interim financial assistance to painters, sculptors, and printmakers facing an unforeseen, catastrophic event such as fire, flood, or a medical emergency. Applicants must have been engaged in a mature phase of their art for at least ten years and must have a current, urgent need that is beyond their means. The maximum award is USD 15,000, with USD 5,000 being typical, given as one-time assistance for a specific emergency. The program does not cover dental work, chronic situations, capital improvements, projects, general indebtedness, or lack of employment. There is no deadline; applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, usually within about four weeks, and within 24 to 48 hours in the most urgent cases.

Open call details

item.title: CERF+ Emergency Relief Grants
item.description:
Organisation: CERF+ (The Artists Safety Net / Craft Emergency Relief Fund)

Location: United States and US Territories

Award: USD 3,000 one-time grant

Duration: One-time grant

Deadline: Rolling / undated

CERF+ provides Emergency Relief Grants of USD 3,000 to craft artists in the United States who have experienced a recent and substantially disruptive career-threatening emergency or disaster. Eligible applicants are individual craft artists aged 18 or older who have lived and worked in the US or US Territories for the past two years and who make original three-dimensional visual art objects by hand, including folk and traditional art forms. The grant is a one-time non-taxable gift with no reporting required on how funds are used. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis, though the program will not fund applicants if too much time has passed since the qualifying event. There is no application fee. Artists may receive one grant per emergency, no more than one per calendar year, and up to four grants over their lifetime.

Open call details

item.title: Innovate Grant
item.description:
Organisation: Innovate Artist Grants

Location: Online (applications accepted worldwide)

Award: USD 1,800 unrestricted grant (two awarded per cycle: one to a visual artist, one to a photographer) USD 35 application fee

Duration: One-time unrestricted award; quarterly cycles (Winter, Spring, Summer, Fall)

Deadline: 17 Sep 2026

Innovate Grant awards two unrestricted USD 1,800 grants every quarter, one to a visual artist and one to a photographer, along with up to 18 honorable mentions who are featured and promoted on the organization's website. The award is unrestricted, so recipients can apply the funds toward new projects, materials, studio rent, travel, or other needs. Grant cycles run four times a year (Winter, Spring, Summer, and Fall). Visual artists and photographers aged 18 and older from anywhere in the world, both US and international, are eligible to apply. Applications require contact details, a grant category, and 3 to 10 images of work, plus a USD 35 application fee. The Spring 2026 cycle closed on June 18, 2026, and the Summer 2026 cycle follows on the regular quarterly schedule.

item.title: TED & POSCA Global Artist Residency 2026: Visible Mending
item.description:
Organisation: TED & POSCA (curated by Fine Acts)

Location: Globally distributed; no single location or central studio. Selected artists work within their own communities, with projects unfolding in public space.

Award: Each selected artist receives a total of USD 15,000 (covering artist fees, production costs/community activation, and documentation), plus a production budget and tools from POSCA, creative direction and storytelling support, and visibility through TED and Fine Acts' global platforms.

Duration: One-year residency term; community-rooted public interventions developed in 2026 for implementation in 2027. The initiative runs for multiple years (2027, 2028, 2029), expanding the cohort year by year.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

TED (the global platform for ideas and storytelling) and POSCA (a creative brand by uni Brands Corporation) are launching a new kind of artist residency: a globally distributed cohort of artists working across different geographies, cultures, and communities, united by a shared drive to shape public life through art. Fine Acts, the external curatorial partner selected by TED & POSCA, leads the curation, creative direction, and coordination. There is no single location and no central studio: selected artists work within their own communities, following a shared creative constraint (a unifying conceptual framework around repair, hope, and beauty), with projects unfolding in public space, community participation as a key part of the work, and process mattering as much as outcome. 2026 THEME, VISIBLE MENDING: inspired in part by Kintsugi (the Japanese practice of repairing broken ceramics with gold), the theme explores repair as a creative and civic act, inviting artists to consider how wounds, ruptures, and imperfections can become sites of transformation, and to make processes of repair visible through public interventions that spark reflection, dialogue, and collective imagination. In 2026, four artists will be selected to develop community-rooted public interventions to be implemented in 2027. AWARD: each selected artist receives a total of USD 15,000 to cover artist fees, production costs/community activation, and documentation, plus a production budget and tools from POSCA, creative direction and storytelling support, and visibility through TED and Fine Acts' global platforms. While the residency term is one year, the initiative will run for multiple years (2027, 2028, 2029), expanding the cohort year by year and building an evolving global archive of civic artistic interventions. Apply by 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Wassaic Project 2027 Summer Exhibition Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: The Wassaic Project

Location: Wassaic, New York, United States (in and around historic Wassaic and Maxon Mills).

Award: A modest honorarium for all accepted artists (part of the organization's effort toward W.A.G.E. certification). Artists creating site-specific installations are also eligible for an Exhibitions Fellowship and a no-fee residency of 1-4 weeks in April or May 2027.

Duration: The 2027 Summer Exhibition runs free and open to the public every weekend, 1 May to 12 September 2027. Optional fellowship residency of 1-4 weeks in April or May 2027.

Deadline: 10 Aug 2026

The Wassaic Project is holding its annual Open Call for the 2027 Summer Exhibition, for artists of all mediums, including 2D work, sculpture, video, new media, site-specific installation, performance, text, poems, essays, and publication-specific work. Selected work is showcased alongside a diverse range of pieces and performances in and around historic Wassaic and Maxon Mills; the 2027 Summer Exhibition will be free and open to the public every weekend from 1 May to 12 September 2027. In an effort toward W.A.G.E. certification, all accepted artists receive a modest honorarium to participate. Artists interested in creating a site-specific installation are also eligible for an Exhibitions Fellowship to help realize their work; Fellows are offered a no-fee residency for 1-4 weeks in April or May 2027 (artists interested in making site-specific work should still apply regardless of whether they are interested in or able to be in residence). The Wassaic Project cultivates and supports community for emerging and professional contemporary artists, writers, and other creatives, with a residency program housed in historic landmark buildings that hosts 10 artists each month in the heart of a rural community. APPLICATION: requires application forms, a CV, and an artwork/portfolio; submitted via SlideRoom. Application fee: USD 25 (no participation fee or additional fees). Deadline 10 August 2026.

Open call details

Award: USD 2,000 artist fee plus a supply stipend. Three artists selected.

Duration: Create original work for a two-day exhibition, 3-4 October 2026.

dadalab is seeking three Austin-based BIPOC artists to create original works exploring extreme heat through the lenses of science, public health and labor. The open call is medium-agnostic and welcomes work in any format. The goal is to bring awareness to environmental justice and the impact of extreme heat in Austin. item.description:
Organisation: Nothing

Location: Anywhere in the world.

Duration: Event, series, or festival to take place later in 2026; winners announced late August 2026.

Club Nothing is a global events series and USD 40,000 fund from the tech brand Nothing, supporting organisers, artists, and collectives creating new music experiences. The fund will award four individuals or collectives USD 10,000 each to throw a club event, series, or festival later this year, backing disruptors bringing new sounds and experiences to their communities through nightlife anywhere in the world. Applications are judged by a panel including DJ and producer Manuka Honey (Marissa Malik), Nothing Global Senior Community Manager and Madruga Festival co-founder Erica Travis, Nothing Global Head of Communications Shavone Charles, Dazed Music Editor Solomon Pace-McCarrick, A&R and music-publishing founder James Supreme, and music-industry professional Layla Mustafa Rock. Applications close 9 August 2026; winners announced late August 2026. item.title: Kala Fellowship Program
item.description:
Organisation: Kala Art Institute

Location: Berkeley, California, USA

Award: USD 3,000 stipend, 24/7 facility access for 1 to 9 months, 1 month of housing for artists outside the 9 Bay Area counties, a Kala class or workshop, and a group exhibition USD 25 application fee (free if applying December through January 15)

Duration: 1 to 9 months within the July 2026 to June 2027 fellowship period

Deadline: 01 Mar 2027

The Kala Fellowship Program supports international, national, and local artists producing innovative work in and across multiple media, with an emphasis on Kala's specialized printmaking, photography, and digital media equipment. The institute supports up to 6 artists per cycle with a USD 3,000 stipend, 24/7 access to its Berkeley facilities for 1 to 9 months, one month of housing for artists based outside the 9 Bay Area counties, a Kala class or workshop, and a culminating group show in the Kala Gallery. Applications are submitted through kala.slideroom.com and require a statement of interest, an artist CV, documentation of 5 to 10 recent works, and two references. Artists enrolled in a degree-granting program during the fellowship period are not eligible. The application opens in December with no fee through January 15, after which a USD 25 application fee applies through the March 1 deadline.

item.title: Roswell Artist-in-Residence Program
item.description:
Organisation: The RAiR Foundation

Location: Roswell, New Mexico, USA

Award: USD 1,100 per month stipend, plus USD 250 per month materials stipend from the Frederick Hammersley Foundation. Includes a private house and a studio with rent, utilities, repairs, and maintenance covered. USD 26.32 application fee

Duration: 12 months (one year)

Deadline: 15 Mar 2027

The Roswell Artist-in-Residence Program, established in 1967 and run by The RAiR Foundation, offers visual artists a year-long studio-based residency known as the Gift of Time. Each resident receives a private house and a studio on a fifty-acre complex, with rent, utilities, repairs, and maintenance covered by the program. Artists also receive a monthly stipend of USD 1,100 plus a USD 250 monthly materials stipend. The program is open to professional visual artists aged 21 and older working in painting, drawing, sculpture, printmaking, photography, video, digital media, and installation. It does not support performance art, writing, music, or production crafts. The application period for the next cycle runs from January 1 to March 15, 2027, and international applicants are accepted with visa support available.

Open call details

item.title: Call to Artists: Walden Fire Station and Library (Calgary Arts Development)
item.description:
Organisation: Calgary Arts Development (CADA)

Location: Walden community, Calgary, Alberta, Canada. Open to local and national (Canadian) professional artists/artist teams.

Award: Total project budget of CAD 275,000 (exclusive of all applicable taxes) for one selected artist/artist team. The artist fee for the total project is CAD 68,750 + GST. Budget breakdown can be adapted to the artist's approach, following CARFAC's recommendation that a minimum 20% of the budget go to the artist's fee; the total allocated budget will not be increased.

Duration: Concept and design from September 2026 to end of July 2027; fabrication and installation (Phase 2) from the second half of 2027 into 2028.

Deadline: 28 Jul 2026

Calgary Arts Development (CADA) is seeking an artist or artist team to join The City of Calgary's Walden Fire Station and Library project team to create permanent public artwork(s) for a combined fire station and library planned for the community of Walden. One artist/artist team will be selected with a total project budget of CAD 275,000 (exclusive of all applicable taxes); the opportunity is open to local and national professional artists/artist teams working in various media. ELIGIBILITY: local and national mid-career to late-career professional public artists and artist teams with a minimum of five years' previous experience creating artwork in the public realm; they must have at least three outdoor permanent public art installations, each with a lifespan of over 10 years and a minimum project budget of CAD 150,000 (see the Canada Council for the Arts definition of a professional artist). Walden does not currently have an active community association, so the project requires experience in artist-led community engagement and a strong understanding of alternative outreach. Artists must be available to meet the project timelines from September 2026 to the end of concept and design by July 2027; fabrication and installation take place in Phase 2, starting in the second half of 2027 and continuing into 2028. REWARD: total project budget CAD 275,000 (artist fee CAD 68,750 + GST); CADA is open to adapting the budget breakdown to the artist's approach, following CARFAC's recommendation of a minimum 20% to the artist's fee, though the total budget will not be increased. TIMELINE: applications open 16 June 2026 (9am MT); pre-recorded information session posted early July; application deadline 28 July 2026 (no applications accepted after 1pm MT); shortlisted interviews mid-September; results late September 2026. Eligibility questions: walden@calgaryartsdevelopment.com.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Studios at MASS MoCA Residencies
item.description:
Organisation: MASS MoCA (Massachusetts Museum of Contemporary Art)

Location: North Adams, Massachusetts, USA

Award: Provides private 24/7 studio space, furnished housing, and seven communal meals per week. Financial aid is available so artists requesting it typically pay between USD 0 and USD 450 per week, and fully funded fellowships cover all residency fees for up to four weeks. Artists who fully self-fund pay a subsidized rate of USD 650 per week.

Duration: 2 or 4 weeks

Deadline: 01 Nov 2026

The Studios at MASS MoCA is a year-round residency program on the museum's campus in North Adams, Massachusetts, hosting up to 10 artists at a time for stays of two or four weeks. It primarily welcomes visual artists across all media and writers in all literary disciplines, and applicants from around the world are encouraged to apply. Residents receive 24-hour access to a private furnished studio, lodging in nearby apartments, and several communal meals each week, plus museum membership benefits and access to The Clark Art Institute. The program offers need-based financial aid (artists typically pay between USD 0 and USD 450 per week) and fully funded fellowships that cover all residency fees for up to four weeks. General application deadlines fall on May 1 and November 1 each year, and the application is free to submit.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Rauschenberg Medical Emergency Grants
item.description:
Organisation: New York Foundation for the Arts (NYFA) / Robert Rauschenberg Foundation

Location: United States (remote application)

Award: One-time grants up to USD 5,000

Duration: One-time grant

Deadline: 17 Nov 2026

The Rauschenberg Medical Emergency Grants, administered by the New York Foundation for the Arts on behalf of the Robert Rauschenberg Foundation, provide one-time grants of up to USD 5,000 to help artists in financial need cover recent unexpected medical, dental, and mental health emergencies. The program is open to generative visual and media artists and choreographers working in visual arts, film, video, electronic and digital arts, and choreography who live anywhere in the United States, the District of Columbia, a Tribal Nation, or a U.S. Territory. Applicants must be artists in financial need, with adjusted gross income at or below USD 80,000 for an individual or USD 160,000 for joint filers, and must not have received a Rauschenberg Medical or Dancer Emergency Grant in the past five years. Eligible expenses include emergency medical bills, diagnostics, consultations, therapy, prescription medications, emergency dental care, and transportation to appointments. The grant does not cover lost wages, general living expenses, or care for anyone other than the artist. Applications are accepted during defined cycles, with the current cycle running roughly from late October to mid November.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Farm, Flock and Fiber Artist in Residence
item.description:
Organisation: A Wrinkle in Thyme Farm

Location: Sumner, Maine, USA

Award: Modest monthly stipend (around USD 400/month) in exchange for roughly 15 to 20 hours/week of farm work, plus free rustic private housing (solar power, wood stove), shared kitchen, bath and WiFi, community meals and farm-grown food, dedicated solo studio time, and weekly collaboration with resident fiber artist Marty Elkin. USD 25 non-refundable application fee

Duration: Six months beginning in May, with an optional two additional months for lambing and early lamb care

Deadline: 31 Dec 2026

A Wrinkle in Thyme Farm is a small, women-owned diversified farm in Sumner, Maine that offers a six-month live-in fiber arts residency starting in May. The resident exchanges an average of 15 to 20 hours per week of farm work for a modest monthly stipend, free private rustic housing, community meals, farm-grown food, and access to the fiber studio. Residents receive solo studio time plus one day a week of learning and collaboration with fiber artist Marty Elkin, working with home-grown Maine wool through felting, dyeing, knitting and spinning. The program also includes training in flock management, animal husbandry and lamb care, with the option to add two months for lambing season. It is designed for emerging fiber artists who are single females able to lift 50 pounds and work around a farm. Applications open in November with a December 31 deadline and a non-refundable USD 25 application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NEIU Art + Design Studio Art Residency 2026-2027
item.description:
Organisation: Northeastern Illinois University (NEIU), Art + Design Department

Location: Salme Harju Steinberg Fine Arts Center, Northeastern Illinois University, Chicago, Illinois, United States. In-person; semester-long access to NEIU facilities.

Award: USD 800 honorarium, plus a semester of full access to NEIU Art + Design facilities specific to the project (Art + Tech, Ceramics, Drawing, Painting, Photography, Printmaking, Sculpture); supplies can be requested with guidance and approval from Studio Area personnel. Visiting Artists may also exhibit in a group exhibition in the NEIU Fine Arts Center Gallery.

Duration: One semester. Residency Session 1: 25 January - 14 May; Session 2: 30 August - 11 December.

Deadline: 09 Aug 2026

The NEIU Art + Design Studio Art Residency strives to create visible connections between Northeastern Illinois University's diverse population of art and design students and working artists who embody these identities, providing insights and inspiration through a dynamic exchange of ideas and techniques. Visiting Artists should demonstrate a commitment to their artistic practice and an interest in engaging with NEIU's students and creative community. Each Visiting Artist is awarded an honorarium and has full access for a semester to the NEIU Art + Design facilities specific to their project; over the residency they showcase their work in a public academic forum, lead a hands-on workshop, and participate in a Portfolio Review Day for NEIU's Art + Design students. FACILITIES (in the Salme Harju Steinberg Fine Arts Center) include a Design Studio with the largest collection of Riso drums at any Chicago university, a Fab Lab (FDM and resin 3D printers, large-format CNC router, vinyl cutters, laser cutters), a Ceramic Studio (throwing wheels, glaze room, electric and gas kilns, raku area), a Letterpress Studio (two Vandercook presses, metal and wood type), a Sculpture Studio (wood shop, clay modeling and spray painting areas), a Photography Studio (analog and digital, plus a lighting studio), and fully equipped Painting, Drawing, and Printmaking studios. BENEFITS: USD 800 honorarium; facility access based on project focus (Art + Tech, Ceramics, Drawing, Painting, Photography, Printmaking, Sculpture), with supplies requestable subject to approval; and the opportunity to exhibit in a group exhibition in the NEIU Fine Arts Center Gallery. PARTICIPATION: lead a 2-hour hands-on workshop for 15-20 students on a topic relevant to the project; give an hour-long artist talk with Q&A; and serve as a guest panelist for a day-long portfolio review. KEY DATES: open call 2 June - 9 August; emailed acceptances in October; Residency Session 1 runs 25 January - 14 May and Session 2 runs 30 August - 11 December. Apply via the JotForm application. Deadline 9 August 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Hayama Artist Residency
item.description:
Organisation: Hayama Artist Residency

Location: Hayama, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan

Award: Fully funded: roundtrip airfare to Tokyo (Narita or Haneda), shared accommodation for 4 weeks, and USD 800 for meals and local transportation. Group exhibition at KOKI ARTS gallery in Tokyo. USD 95 application fee for first-time applicants

Duration: 4 weeks

Deadline: 30 Sep 2026

The Hayama Artist Residency introduces visual artists from around the world to Japanese culture and offers them their first gallery exhibition in Japan. The four-week residency takes place in Hayama, a coastal town in Kanagawa Prefecture about 1.5 hours from Tokyo. Selected artists receive roundtrip airfare to Tokyo, shared accommodation with one other resident, and USD 800 for meals and local transportation. A core element is a group exhibition during the stay, hosted in partnership with KOKI ARTS, a contemporary gallery in the Bakurocho art district of Tokyo. The program is open to visual artists over 21 working in any medium, based anywhere in the world, and applications are reviewed by the director and an international selection committee. The June 2027 cycle has an application deadline of September 30, 2026, with notification by December 15, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Eamonn McCabe Bursary 2026 (Royal Photographic Society)
item.description:
Organisation: The Royal Photographic Society (RPS), in partnership with Eamonn McCabe's family and The Guardian

Location: International bursary. Applicants must be aged 30 years or under on the closing date. Interviews held via Zoom.

Award: GBP 3,000 to deliver a photography project on the theme of 'sporting endeavour'. The completed project will be published by The Guardian and the RPS, who will work with the recipient to bring the project to a wider audience.

Duration: The project should be completed within one year of being awarded.

Deadline: 03 Aug 2026

The Eamonn McCabe Bursary, launched in 2024, commemorates the life of photojournalist Eamonn McCabe (1948-2022), who made his name as a sports photographer before moving to wider editorial work, mainly for The Observer and The Guardian, and who supported a generation of photographers from the 1980s to the 2020s. Run by the RPS in partnership with McCabe's family and The Guardian, this international bursary continues that legacy. AWARD: GBP 3,000 to a photographer aged 30 years or under to deliver a photography project responding to the theme of 'sporting endeavour', which can be interpreted as widely as the applicant wishes, though the general approach should be suitable for publication. The project should normally be new, not previously exhibited or published. As well as professional sports, the call particularly encourages projects covering grassroots and community activities (for example, addressing issues such as mental health or supporting displaced communities), and welcomes creative interpretations of the theme. The completed project will be published by The Guardian and the RPS. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be aged 30 years or under on the closing date. PROCESS: interviews via Zoom in September; recipient announced by the end of September; project completed within one year of award. Apply via the RPS application portal using RPS Account credentials. Closing date 3 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Prisma Art Prize 20th Edition
item.description:
Organisation: Prisma Art Prize

Location: Rome, Italy

Award: EUR 2,000 annual cash fund distributed across quarterly winners, plus group exhibition at Palazzo Merulana, inclusion in the collective catalogue, artist interviews, and eligibility for solo show prizes (Isorrophia Gallery, Pallavicini-Dettori) and partner art residencies (Dar Meso, Casacon, Osservatoriomaree) EUR 34 entry fee for 1 to 3 artworks, plus EUR 5 for each additional artwork up to a maximum of 8

Duration: Group exhibition runs November 5 to 8, 2026

Deadline: 10 Sep 2026

The Prisma Art Prize is an international award for painting and two-dimensional artistic research based in Rome. The 20th edition selects emerging and established artists working in painting, drawing, illustration, printmaking, and traditional mixed media for a group exhibition at Palazzo Merulana, opening November 5, 2026. Winners share an annual cash fund of EUR 2,000 distributed across quarterly calls and receive inclusion in the collective catalogue plus artist interviews. Additional opportunities include solo exhibition prizes and fully funded placements at partner art residencies. The prize is open internationally to all living artists regardless of age, gender, nationality, or educational background. The submission deadline for this edition is September 10, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Textile Exchange x Atmos: Fashion Regenerated 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Textile Exchange (with Atmos)

Location: Open call; work to be promoted globally through Textile Exchange and Atmos and exhibited at the 2026 Textile Exchange conference.

Award: Overall winner: a paid collaborative commission worth USD 10,000 with Textile Exchange to develop a new body of work documenting the evidence of regeneration, published through Atmos and Textile Exchange and shown at the 2026 conference. Two runners-up: work promoted by Textile Exchange and displayed at the conference exhibition, plus a USD 2,000 cash prize.

Duration: One-off commission/prize.

Deadline: 20 Jul 2026

Fashion Regenerated is an open call from Textile Exchange and Atmos. Winners are selected by a panel of expert judges and, for the first time, a public vote. The shortlist is promoted through Textile Exchange and Atmos, amplifying inspirational stories to an engaged audience of thousands. The overall winner receives a paid collaborative commission to the value of USD 10,000 with Textile Exchange to develop a new body of work documenting the evidence of regeneration, working closely with the team to identify the right story and access; the winning work is published through Atmos, Textile Exchange, and shown to an audience of more than 1,300 changemakers at the 2026 Textile Exchange conference. Two runners-up have their work promoted by Textile Exchange and displayed at the conference exhibition, and there is also a USD 2,000 cash prize. Check the call site for full eligibility and submission details. Deadline: 20 July 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Illuminations Grant for Black Trans Women Visual Artists
item.description:
Organisation: Queer|Art

Location: United States; open to self-identified Black trans women and trans femmes working in visual art and based in the US.

Award: USD 10,000 to one winning artist, plus USD 1,250 each to four distinguished finalists. Winners and finalists also receive additional professional development resources and guidance, and each judge conducts a studio visit with the winning artist.

Duration: One-time award

Deadline: Closed 02 Jul 2026

Now in its 7th year, the Illuminations Grant for Black Trans Women Visual Artists is an annual USD 10,000 grant from Queer|Art that provides critical support to Black trans women whose work has often been under-recognized in the visual art field. To further recognize finalists for their artistic achievements, Queer|Art also provides a USD 1,250 award to four distinguished finalists. Winning artists and finalists receive additional professional development resources and further guidance to bolster their creative development. The grant was developed and named in partnership with Mariette Pathy Allen, Aaryn Lang, and Serena Jara, and is made possible entirely through support from visual artist Mariette Pathy Allen, whose photographic work over the last forty years has focused on expanding cultural consciousness around gender and transformation. It is administered through Queer|Art with a rotating panel of judges, each of whom conducts a studio visit with the winning artist as part of the award's focus on creative and professional development; judges for the 2026 cycle include Jordyn Jay, Isis Awad, and Utē Petit. ELIGIBILITY: qualified artists must be self-identified Black trans women and trans femmes working in visual art and based in the United States. Deadline: July 2, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: XENO Prize for Artists' Books
item.description:
Organisation: Franklin Furnace

Location: United States.

Award: USD 5,000 to one artist to publish one book on the topic of book banning and/or burning, in an edition of at least 100 copies.

Duration: One-time prize

Deadline: Closed 04 Jul 2026

In pursuit of its mission to present, preserve, interpret, educate, and advocate on behalf of avant-garde art, especially forms that may be vulnerable due to institutional neglect, cultural bias, their ephemeral nature, or politically unpopular content, Franklin Furnace offers the annual XENO Prizes for Artists' Books. One artist will receive USD 5,000 to publish one book on the topic of book banning and/or burning in an edition of at least 100 copies. Deadline: July 4, 2026.

Open call details
